Guideline: Formal register (vouvoiement). Uses vous consistently throughout the UI. Tu/toi must never appear.
Professional, precise, and neutral. Avoids sensationalizing pay gap data. Register comparable to a financial audit report.
Use gender-neutral phrasing where possible. Avoid defaulting to masculine generics. Inclusive writing only when explicitly requested by the client.
Sentence case throughout. Only the first word and proper nouns are capitalized. Title case must never be used on UI labels or headings.
French guillemets with non-breaking spaces: « example ». Do not use straight double quotes or curly quotes.
Non-breaking space before: ? ! : ; « — Standard French typography rules apply. Percent: 15 % (space before sign).
{variable} placeholders preserved unchanged. Decimal separator: comma (,). Thousands: space. Date: DD/MM/YYYY.
Preposition-linked noun phrases: Analyse de l'équité salariale, Groupe de comparaison, Bande de rémunération.
Kept in English: PayAnalytics, benchmark, KPI, p-value, QR code, API, CSV, feedback. Translated: compensation -> Rémunération (false friend — never use 'compensation').
Infinitive for action buttons: Ajouter un filtre, Exporter la liste, Comparer. Verb placed first, followed by article + noun.
Actions: Ajouter un [noun], Supprimer le [noun], Exporter la [noun]. Confirmations: Souhaitez-vous vraiment... ? Empty states: Aucun(e) ... pour le moment.

Pay gap=Écart salarial; Adjusted pay gap=Écart salarial ajusté; Unadjusted pay gap=Écart salarial non ajusté; Gender pay gap=Écart salarial hommes-femmes; Pay band=Bande de rémunération; Salary range=Fourchette salariale; Compensation=Rémunération; Raise=Augmentation; Benchmark=Benchmark (kept); Pay equity=Équité salariale; Equal pay=Égalité salariale; Job evaluation=Évaluation de l'emploi; Workforce analytics=Analyse du personnel; Headcount=Effectif; Dataset=Jeu de données; Comparison group=Groupe de comparaison; Employee group=Groupe d'employés; Maternity leave=Congé maternité; Bonus=Prime; Compa-ratio=Compa-ratio (kept)
+32% average length vs. English. Prefer concise equivalents; avoid padding.
